sutta: monks there are these 8 persons worthy of offerings worthy of gifts worthy of oblations deserving to be reverently saluted the world's fearless field for married 1 8 the stream winner that's number 1 number 2 he who has entered the path for the realization of the fruit of stream winning number 3 the once returner or sakadagami number 4 he who attains to the path for the realization thereof number 5 the non-returner or anagamin number 6 he who attains he who has entered the path for the realization of the fruit of non-returning i number 7 the arahant and number 8 he was entered on the path to arahantship that's the end of the
commentary: so here the buddha is talking about 8 persons who are aryas and you notice it is 8 persons not 4 persons because in the abi dharma the path attainment is supposed to be followed immediately the next moment by the fruition but this is not so in the sutas in the sutas you have uh 8 persons which means that a path attainer actually exists for you to make off rings too so it does appear in the success that path attainment and fruition attainment can take quite some time their partner quite some time apart not necessarily 1 following the other immediately the other thing you notice is that these terms sutapanas are all fruition attainers they're all fruition attainers here in this book they have translated wrongly they have translated the stream winner and he who attains to the realization of the fruit of stream winning so from this translation it appears like the stream winner sutapana
